fair_value_today.basis: base case (Metis) intrinsic value — the anchor, not a weighted blend

fair_value_12m.basis: #1 rolled forward at r=10.5% less dividend 70

detail.multiple_rationale: A defensible traded P/B of 1.20x in 12 months represents a PARTIAL mean-reversion from 0.93x today toward the 2.18x intrinsic justified P/B. The 1.20x is earned by: (1) CONTRACTED: the 10%+ dividend yield anchors institutional holders and provides a cash return floor, limiting re-rating risk; (2) CONTRACTED: BFIN's NI trajectory in Q2-Q4 2026 should show sequential improvement from the Q1 trough (provision seasonality, partial rate easing). The gap between 1.20x and the intrinsic 2.18x is the part of the thesis that requires a full multi-year cycle to play out -- we do not assume full convergence in 12 months. At 1.20x P/B, the stock would trade at 7.7x corrected NTM EPS -- a very modest re-rating vs the current 7.2x, well below the pre-crisis ~10x P/E. The street consensus is 860 IDR (P/B 1.19x current BV), which our target is close to but expressed on forward BV. We are NOT assuming the market re-rates to intrinsic -- we are assuming it moves from 'panic price' (0.93x) to 'modest-recovery price' (1.20x).

street_reconstruction.what_price_implies: At 690 IDR (P/B 0.955x), the market prices in a persistent crisis: either ROE stays at ~13.5% (with no recovery from the 2022-2025 compression) at a crisis Ke of 14%, OR ROE recovers to ~10% at our fundamental Ke of 10.5%. The former is the market's most plausible implicit assumption. The sub-book valuation is NOT pricing a default or NPF blowout -- Fitch AA-(idn) stable and NPF 1.25% (vs sector 2.70%) make distress remote. It is pricing a 'new normal' of lower ROE and higher Ke in the rate-elevated Indonesian macro environment.
street_reconstruction.what_street_implies: The mean analyst target of 860 IDR (P/B 1.19x) implies Ke of ~13% (the pre-hike baseline from 2024) and ROE stable at 14-15%. The street has not updated its Ke assumption for the 100bp BI rate surprise in May-June 2026. UBS (the sole sell-side bear, PT 745) is more internally consistent: it has updated its credit cost and growth forecasts for the adverse environment. The 2 buy + 1 hold analysts are implicitly using stale Ke assumptions.
street_reconstruction.history_anchor: BFIN historically traded at 1.0x-1.8x P/B (2018-2022 range) when ROE was 18-22%. The 0.93x P/B is the lowest level in recent history, consistent with a significant repricing from peak. The P/B-to-ROE relationship has been consistent: 20% ROE commanded 1.5-1.8x P/B; 15% ROE commanded 1.0-1.2x P/B. The current 14.8% ROE should logically command ~1.0x P/B at pre-crisis Ke -- and the stock is at 0.93x P/B. The crisis Ke is the overhang.
street_reconstruction.contested_lever: Ke is the crux, not ROE. The market and our fundamental Ke disagree by 350bp (14% vs 10.5%). This Ke gap alone moves the justified P/B from 0.93x (where the stock trades) to 2.18x (our fundamental). ROE recovery is the second lever -- the market assumes flat ROE; we assume recovery to 16.5%. But the Ke disagreement is larger in value terms.
street_reconstruction.our_disagree: We disagree with the market on TWO fronts: (1) Ke should normalize from crisis 14% to fundamental 10.5% as BI hike cycle ends and rupiah stabilizes; (2) ROE will recover toward 16.5% as provision normalizes and GP margin recovers. Both disagreements favor the BUY. We partially agree with the street (860 IDR mean target) on the near-term traded level -- our 12m target of 910 IDR is close to the street's 860 IDR, because neither the street nor we expect full intrinsic value realization in 12 months.
street_reconstruction.ubs_engage: UBS's bear thesis (PT 745, Sell) is the most rigorous dissent. UBS argues credit costs of 4.6-5.1% for 2025-27 -- our base uses 2.5% of receivables (lower). Q1 2026 credit cost data will be the first real test: if Q2 2026 cost of credit annualizes above 4%, UBS is right and our normalization thesis is wrong. Our counterargument: the Q1 2025 credit cost spike that triggered UBS's downgrade (4.4%, up 160bp QoQ) was partly seasonal and partly driven by the post-COVID restructuring vintage burning through. The 9M 2024 NPF at 1.25% gross (best in sector) is not consistent with sustained 4.6-5.1% credit costs. UBS's 2.5% loan CAGR is already contradicted by FY2025 new financing +9.3%.

conviction.label: MEDIUM
conviction.basis: action BUY: Daedalus assigned MEDIUM (evidence read across base/bull/bear) -> MEDIUM

sensitivity_matrix.metric: implied P/B x BV = IDR per share
sensitivity_matrix.dominant_lever: Ke is the dominant lever in the justified P/B framework. A 100bp change in Ke moves the value by 200-400 IDR/share at current ROE levels. ROE is second-order: a 100bp change in ROE moves the value by ~150-200 IDR/share. The call is primarily a bet on Ke normalization from crisis 14% to fundamental 10.5%.
sensitivity_matrix.note: Current price 690 IDR = P/B 0.955x. At Ke=10.5%, ROE=14%: 1,228 IDR (77% upside). At Ke=11.5%, ROE=14%: 1,023 IDR (48% upside). All cells above current price -- asymmetric risk/reward.

bridge_logic: #1 fair value today 1357.03 -> #3 fair value 12m 1430.2 (x(1+0.105) - div 70); #4 target 12m 909.72 (defensible_multiple); implied return to #4 = 42.0%, to #3 = 117.4%, to street mean 860 = 24.6%
